title: Smoky Creamy Shandong Cannellini Beans Dumplings
description: A nostalgic Shandong-inspired dish featuring creamy cannellini beans and smoky flavors, served in a delicate dumpling wrapper. A kosher-friendly twist on traditional Chinese cuisine.

ingredients.name: Smoked soybean paste
ingredients.notes: Available at Asian grocery stores or online

ingredients.name: Scallions
ingredients.notes: Chopped, green and white parts separated

ingredients.name: Dumpling wrappers
ingredients.notes: Available at Asian grocery stores or online

ingredients.name: Creamy cashew sauce
ingredients.notes: See recipe below for cashew sauce

instructions: In a medium pan, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Add the cooked cannellini beans, smoked soybean paste, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the flavors are well combined.
Meanwhile, prepare the dumpling wrappers according to package instructions.
To assemble the dumplings, lay a dumpling wrapper on a clean surface. Place a tablespoon of the cannellini bean mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with water, then fold the wrapper into a half-moon shape, pressing the edges to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Pan-fry the dumplings in a large skillet with the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the dumplings with the creamy cashew sauce drizzled on top. Garnish with chopped green scallions and serve immediately.

To make the cashew sauce, blend the following ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth: 1/2 cup soaked cashews, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon honey, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, and 1 tablespoon water.
